summary: Public companies that put crypto (mostly BTC, later ETH/SOL) on their balance sheet move prices through three repeatable patterns. First, the initial "we're becoming a treasury company" announcement reliably rockets the company's own STOCK (Semler ~+30-37%, Metaplanet thousands of %, SharpLink roughly +1,800% to +2,600%) while only mildly nudging the coin. Second, sustained accumulation funded by issuing stock/debt at a premium to net asset value (mNAV) acts as a steady demand pump that absorbs supply during bull phases. Third — the dangerous mirror image — when the coin falls and the mNAV premium collapses toward or below 1.0, these leveraged holders face distribution and "death-spiral" forced-selling risk that adds reflexive sell-pressure (Tesla's 2022 $936M dump; the Nov 2025 BTC ~-15% slide amid treasury-firm stress).

archetypes.name: Treasury-Adoption Announcement (company pivots to crypto reserve)
archetypes.mechanism: A public company announces it is adopting BTC/ETH as a primary treasury reserve asset, usually copying the MicroStrategy playbook. The other side of the trade is equity investors who suddenly re-rate the stock as a leveraged, tax-advantaged crypto proxy (and short-sellers who get squeezed). The stock pops violently because the announcement converts a sleepy small-cap into a high-beta crypto vehicle that can trade at a large premium to the crypto it holds (mNAV > 1). The underlying coin moves much less — the announced first purchase is small relative to coin market cap and is often already bought before disclosure — so the coin reaction is mild-bullish to neutral, driven by sentiment/adoption narrative rather than flow.
affected_scope: other treasury-stock peers (MSTR, SMLR, MARA, SBET)
archetypes.typical_duration: stock pop intraday-to-days; narrative read-across can persist weeks; coin effect <24h
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: +20% to +4000%+ on the announcing stock (size-dependent); BTC/ETH typically -2% to +3% same day
archetypes.recurrence: very frequent in 2024-2025 — dozens of copycats; cluster events around each new high-profile entrant
leading_signals: 8-K / press release announcing treasury policy adoption
convertible-note or ATM equity raise filed days before
small beaten-down small-cap with a crypto-aligned chairman

historical_instances.trigger: Bitmine Immersion (BMNR) reaches 5,416,901 ETH (~4.49% of supply) - the largest corporate ETH treasury (MSTR of ETH).
historical_instances.description: Bitmine Immersion Technologies (BMNR, Tom Lee-linked) accumulated to 5,416,901 ETH (~4.49% of the ~120.7M supply, ~$10.9B at ~$2,003) by May 31 2026, plus 203 BTC and equity stakes - ~$11.6B total - pursuing an 'Alchemy of 5%' ETH-supply goal. It grew from ~4.1M ETH (Jan) to 5.4M (May), accumulating aggressively even as ETH fell ~40% YTD. The ETH analog of MicroStrategy's BTC flywheel: a structural demand sink, no clean single-day catalyst, with circular reflexivity/leverage risk if ETH keeps falling.

price_impacts.pct_change: structural demand (no clean single-day move; ETH fell ~40% YTD regardless)

historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: 5.42M ETH (~4.49% of supply) accumulated; largest single corporate ETH holder.

historical_instances.trigger: SpaceX S-1 IPO filing discloses 18,712 BTC (fair value $1.293B).
historical_instances.description: SpaceX's S-1 (filed 2026-05-20, ahead of a record ~$1.75T IPO under ticker SPCX; roadshow ~Jun 4, trading ~Jun 12 2026) disclosed 18,712 BTC at fair value $1.293B / cost basis ~$661M (avg ~$35,300) as of Mar 31 2026 — more than double prior Arkham estimates. Notably SpaceX originally bought 25,724 BTC in 2021 and quietly sold ~27% down to 18,712 (corporate holders are not all diamond-hands). Ranks ~7th-8th among corporate holders (varies by source/methodology), ahead of Tesla (~11,509), Coinbase, and Block (~9,000), far behind Strategy's 843,706. The S-1 also disclosed a binding ~$45B compute agreement with Anthropic ($1.25B/mo through May 2029) for COLOSSUS/COLOSSUS II (post-xAI acquisition). A private-company disclosure of LEGACY holdings (no new buying) — no isolable BTC impact; BTC -2.3% over the window was the broad May-2026 downtrend. Tesla-SpaceX merger talks (May 2026) would create a combined ~30,221 BTC #5 holder.

price_impacts.window: May 18-22 2026 (broad downtrend; no isolable disclosure impact)

historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Disclosure only (no purchase); reveals a previously-private holder + a past ~7,000 BTC sell-down.

historical_instances.trigger: GameStop announces it bought 4,710 BTC (~$500M), first major crypto push; funded via $1.5B April convertible notes
historical_instances.description: Counter-example: when the market already doubts the vehicle (dilution fears, meme-stock skepticism), the adoption pop FAILS — GME fell on the day despite the BTC buy. Sources diverge on magnitude: TradingView's recap shows a ~-3% close after gapping up, while another report cites -10.9% intraday after a +4.4% pre-market. Direction (down) is well-supported; exact magnitude is uncertain, so the range is widened.

historical_instances.trigger: SharpLink Gaming (SBET) announces pivot to an ETH treasury vehicle (Joe Lubin chairman)
historical_instances.description: Extended the playbook from BTC to ETH. SBET spiked enormously from its base (sources range from ~+1,800% per Bybit, climbing ~$6.70 to ~$124, to ~+2,600% per ts2.tech) before later giving back ~86-92% from peak. Kicked off a wave of ETH treasury vehicles (later BitMine) that helped drive ETH +53% over late-June to mid-July 2025. Note: the ETH +53.3% window is a cohort-flywheel effect, not directly attributable to the SBET announcement day.

historical_instances.trigger: Semler Scientific (med-tech) announces BTC as primary treasury reserve, buys 581 BTC for ~$40M
historical_instances.description: Classic small-cap re-rate. A beaten-down ~$210M med-tech (down >30% YTD) became a BTC proxy overnight; stock soared while BTC barely moved — textbook stock-pops-coin-flat signature. Perplexity puts the day's close gain at ~30%; the +37% figure appears to reflect an intraday peak. BTC roughly flat to slightly down.

historical_instances.trigger: Metaplanet (Japan) announces it is adopting BTC as a treasury reserve asset; begins accumulation
historical_instances.description: Japanese 'MicroStrategy' birth (date 2024-04-08 confirmed). Stock ran thousands of percent over the following year on individual buy announcements; sources put the ~1-year gain anywhere from ~+4,000% to ~+8,850% depending on the endpoint chosen, so treat the precise multiple as approximate. The most extreme example of stock-side re-rating; coin effect negligible per-event.

historical_instances.trigger: Tesla discloses $1.5B BTC purchase (bought in January at ~$34,700 avg) and plans to accept BTC as payment
historical_instances.description: Largest, highest-profile corporate adoption to date. Unlike most adoptions, Tesla's size + Musk halo moved the COIN hard, not just the stock — BTC jumped ~14.5% same day (roughly from the low-$39,000s to over $44,000), hitting a new ATH. Corrected from the prior '+15% to +20%' range, which overstated the move per Perplexity/bitbo price data.

historical_instances.trigger: MicroStrategy announces first BTC purchase, 21,454 BTC for $250M at ~$11,653, calling BTC 'superior to cash' — the template for the entire category
historical_instances.description: First major US public-company treasury adoption. MSTR re-rated as a BTC proxy; launched the playbook every later entrant copied. BTC reaction muted; the durable effect was the template itself. Perplexity-confirmed: MSTR rose ~9% (from ~$123.62 to ~$134.89); BTC roughly flat same day.

archetypes.name: Sustained Accumulation / mNAV-Premium Flywheel (ongoing buys funded at a premium)
archetypes.mechanism: Once established, a treasury company runs a reflexive flywheel: because its stock trades at a premium to the crypto it holds (mNAV > 1), it issues equity/convertibles/preferreds ABOVE net asset value and uses the cash to buy more coin. Each buy is accretive (raises crypto-per-share), validating the premium, which lets it raise more — net new dry powder flowing INTO the coin. The other side is dilutive buyers of the stock who accept a premium for leveraged exposure, and coin sellers who get a persistent structural bid. Per individual buy the coin barely moves (each purchase is small vs daily volume), but in aggregate the cohort is a large, price-insensitive, sustained buyer. DATCos poured an estimated ~$42.7B into crypto in 2025 ($22.6B in Q3 alone per cited estimates), a real demand sink during the bull phase.
archetypes.typical_lag: diffuse — builds over weeks/quarters rather than per-event
archetypes.typical_duration: persists as long as mNAV > ~1 and capital markets stay open (multi-quarter)
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: individually negligible (<1-2% per buy); cohort contributes meaningfully to multi-month uptrends
archetypes.recurrence: continuous through 2024-2025 bull phase; weekly purchase 8-Ks from MSTR and peers
leading_signals: growing count of new treasury entrants (crowding)
exchange outflows to corporate custody addresses

historical_instances.trigger: Strategy (MSTR) reaches 843,706 BTC — ~3/4 of all corporate-held BTC; nearing its 1M target.
historical_instances.description: Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy) holds 843,706 BTC as of Jun 1 2026 (avg cost $66,385; ~$33.1B), ~3/4 of all corporate-treasury BTC and the largest corporate holder by far (e.g. >40x the largest private-company stacks). Late-April 2026 it added 3,273 BTC (~$255M) at 818,334. Ongoing accumulation — a structural demand sink, not a single-day catalyst.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: 843,706 BTC held; persistent ATM-funded accumulation toward a 1M-BTC target.

historical_instances.trigger: Q3 2025: Digital Asset Treasury Companies (DATCos) estimated to deploy ~$22.6B into crypto in a single quarter (of ~$42.7B for full-year 2025, per cited estimates)
historical_instances.description: Quantifies the flywheel at cohort scale. This wall of premium-funded buying supported BTC's climb to the >$126k October peak before the premium unwind began. The dollar-deployment figures are third-party estimates, not audited totals, hence medium confidence; the BTC trend move is approximate.

price_impacts.pct_change: to >$126k peak (~+100% from early-2025 lows; approximate)

historical_instances.trigger: ETH-treasury accumulation wave: SharpLink + new entrants (BitMine, Tom Lee) ramp ETH buying through summer 2025
historical_instances.description: Cohort flywheel applied to ETH. Aggregate treasury demand coincided with ETH +53% over late-June to mid-July 2025 (CoinGecko-measured); BitMine ultimately accumulated ~3-5% of all ETH supply, a major structural demand sink.

historical_instances.trigger: MicroStrategy buys ~27,200 BTC for ~$2.03B (Oct 31-Nov 10) at ~$74,463 avg; part of post-election mega-accumulation
historical_instances.description: Peak-flywheel example: equity-funded multi-billion buys during BTC's run to $100k+, with mNAV premium reported near ~4x in late 2024. The cohort's relentless premium-funded bid coincided with the strongest leg of the 2024 bull market. The BTC and MSTR moves below are multi-week trend figures, not attributable to this single 8-K.

historical_instances.trigger: Marathon Digital (MARA) adopts full 'HODL' policy — retains all mined BTC and buys on open market (e.g. ~4,144 BTC / ~$249M in Aug 2024 at ~$59,500)
historical_instances.description: Miner-turned-accumulator variant: converts a flow seller (miners normally sell coin to cover costs) into a holder/buyer, removing sell-pressure (supply-restriction) and adding demand. Stock becomes a leveraged BTC play. Price impacts are qualitative/structural rather than a measured single-event move; left as estimates.

archetypes.name: Distribution / Forced Selling / mNAV-Discount Unwind (the flywheel in reverse)
archetypes.mechanism: The reflexive flywheel runs backward. When the coin falls and the stock's premium collapses to or below net asset value (mNAV <= 1), accretive equity issuance stops, and pressure builds to monetize: either an outright corporate sale (Tesla 2022) or, for over-leveraged firms with debt covenants, forced liquidation to defend mNAV / service debt. Selling pushes the coin down, which drops more firms' mNAV below 1, forcing more sales — a 'death spiral.' The other side is whoever absorbs the supply at distressed prices. Direct corporate sales tend to produce a modest, sentiment-driven coin dip (the sale is often already done), but the SYSTEMIC risk — a cohort holding a large share of supply being forced out together — is the real bearish tail, amplified by leveraged longs in the treasury stocks unwinding simultaneously.
affected_scope: treasury-stock cohort (MSTR, MARA, SBET, BMNR, Metaplanet)
archetypes.typical_lag: sentiment dip immediate; systemic forced-selling plays out over days-to-weeks
archetypes.typical_duration: single-sale dip hours-to-days; mNAV-unwind regimes persist weeks-to-months
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: -2% to -5% coin on a single disclosed corporate sale; -10% to -25% coin during a full premium-collapse/forced-selling regime
archetypes.recurrence: rare for outright sales (Strategy held through all of 2023-2025); systemic discount-unwind episodes clustered in late 2025
leading_signals: treasury stock down >50% YoY while coin down less (premium gone)
'one in three treasuries below NAV' breadth
rising correlation of treasury stocks crashing together

historical_instances.trigger: Strategy (MSTR) discloses its first BTC SALE since 2022 - 32 BTC (~$2.5M) for a preferred-dividend payment.
historical_instances.description: Strategy's 8-K (Jun 1 2026) disclosed selling just 32 BTC (~$2.5M at ~$77,135) between May 26-31 to fund preferred-stock distributions - the first confirmed sale since Dec 2022 and a microscopic 0.0038% of its 843,706-BTC stack. Materially trivial, but the 'Saylor never-sell era cracks' NARRATIVE rattled the market: BTC fell ~4.5% to ~$69,800 (Jun 1-2; part of the broader June-2026 risk-off to ~$63k) amid $2.43B of May spot-BTC-ETF outflows (largest of 2026). A textbook case of a symbolically-loaded but fundamentally-immaterial event moving price via narrative, not flow - the inverse of the accumulation flywheel's reflexivity.

price_impacts.window: Jun 1-2 2026 (8-K disclosure, to ~$69,800; broader June crash to ~$63k)

historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: 32 BTC sold (~$2.5M, 0.0038% of holdings) for a dividend; $2.43B May BTC-ETF outflows (largest of 2026).

historical_instances.trigger: Trump Media transfers 2,650 BTC (~$205M) to Crypto.com (says 'did not sell').
historical_instances.description: Trump Media & Technology Group moves 2,650 BTC (~$205M) to Crypto.com, its 2nd large exchange transfer, amid steep losses (bought 11,542 BTC at avg $118,522, down ~$455M; Q1 net loss $405.9M; withdrew its spot-BTC-ETF application). Feared as distribution, but no isolable impact — BTC +0.44% over the window. Illustrates that exchange transfers != confirmed sells and that small-treasury distress is noise vs market.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: 2,650 BTC to a CEX (custody/liquidity/possible-sale ambiguous; company says not sold).

historical_instances.trigger: SharpLink (SBET) ETH-treasury vehicle down ~86-92% from peak; ETH-treasury cohort de-rates as premiums vanish
historical_instances.description: Shows the unwind extends to the ETH cohort. The same vehicles that amplified ETH's summer rally became forced/willing distributors and de-rating stocks as mNAV collapsed, with BitMine sitting on multi-billion paper losses. SBET drawdown from peak is ~86% (ts2.tech) to ~92.5% (Bybit); the BitMine ~$8.66B paper-loss figure is a single forward-dated (2026) source, so this instance stays medium/low confidence.

price_impacts.pct_change: sharp decline (BitMine ~$8.66B paper loss, per single 2026 source)

historical_instances.trigger: Strategy CEO Phong Le comments on conditions under which the firm would sell BTC; reports note MSTR market cap falling near/below its BTC holdings, with MSTR down sharply YoY (~-45% to -63% per varying sources)
historical_instances.description: Sentiment-shock on the mere prospect of the largest holder selling. BTC traded toward/below $86k in early December; demonstrates how reflexive-deleveraging fear in the treasury cohort transmits to spot even without an actual sale (Strategy did not sell BTC in 2025). The precise '-60% YoY' is endpoint-dependent — Perplexity cites figures from ~-45% (from Nov peak) to ~-63% (1-year), so a range is used and confidence stays medium. The 'below $86k toward ~$82k' figure is approximate/web-sourced.

historical_instances.trigger: Strategy (MSTR) mNAV (market cap to BTC NAV) falls below 1.0 for the first time as BTC slides from ~$107k toward $95k; reports cite roughly one-in-three treasury firms dropping below NAV
historical_instances.description: First time the category leader's NAV multiple slipped below 1.0 (corroborated by Cointelegraph/TradingView coverage around 2025-11-10/11-12; it recovered above ~1.09 shortly after). The earlier '0.97x' precision was unsourced and is removed; the exact sub-1 low is not published. This removed accretive issuance and crystallized 'death-spiral' fears — the regime flip from flywheel to unwind, with treasury stocks selling off as a correlated cohort. BTC move is CoinGecko-measured.

historical_instances.trigger: BTC tops ~$126k (early Oct) then rolls over; premium-funded buying stalls as mNAVs compress across the cohort
historical_instances.description: The top of the 2025 flywheel and start of the unwind. As the premium that fueled accumulation evaporated, the marginal treasury bid disappeared, removing structural support. BTC move CoinGecko-measured for the Oct 5-15 window.

historical_instances.trigger: Tesla reveals it converted ~75% of its BTC to fiat in Q2 2022 (~$936M, ~30k BTC sold at ~$29k avg), citing China-COVID cash needs
historical_instances.description: The canonical corporate distribution event. Reaction was modest because the sale was already executed during the quarter and was framed as idiosyncratic (cash management, not a BTC view) — BTC dipped then recovered. Perplexity confirms the direction (BTC fell) but did not corroborate an exact same-day percentage from one source; the widely reported ~-2% intraday figure is retained as approximate. Confirms single disclosed sales are weak coin catalysts vs the announcement-buy asymmetry.
